'''Denny Solomona''' (born 27 September 1993) is a former professional [[rugby football]]er who played [[rugby union]] as a [[Wing (rugby union)|winger]] for  [[England national rugby union team|England]] at international level. He previously played rugby union for [[Sale Sharks]] in [[Premiership Rugby]] and played [[rugby league]] as a winger for the [[Melbourne Storm]] Under-20s, [[London Broncos]] and the [[Castleford Tigers]]. He played for [[Samoa national rugby league team|Samoa]] at international rugby league.

==Youth==
Solomona was born and brought up in [[Auckland]], and was educated first at [[Otahuhu College|Ōtāhuhu College]] then at [[St Peter's College, Auckland|St Peter's College]] where he was a member of the school's Rugby Union First XV and won the Boris Shroj Cup in 2010 as the top try scorer.<ref>''St Peter's College Magazine 2010'', p. 96.</ref>

==Rugby League==
In 2012, Solomona became a member of the [[Melbourne Storm]] Rugby League franchise being recruited out of Melbourne's youth system. He only played 13 games during the 2012 [[National Youth Competition (rugby league)|National Youth Competition]] season, with a fractured [[fibula]] in Round 13. Nevertheless, the 19-year-old scored eight tries from those 13 games. Solomona joined the Melbourne Storm's senior squad in [[2013 Melbourne Storm season|2013]].<ref>[http://www.melbournestorm.com.au/nycplayerprofiledisplay/Storm-NYC/Denny%20Solomona/4813 Denny Solomona, Official Website of the Melbourne Storm] {{webarchive|url=https://web.archive.org/web/20131029191938/http://www.melbournestorm.com.au/nycplayerprofiledisplay/Storm-NYC/Denny%20Solomona/4813 |date=2013-10-29}} (Retrieved 9 July 2013)</ref>

Solomona signed for [[Super League]] side [[London Broncos]] in January 2014 after being released from his contract with [[Melbourne Storm]].<ref>{{cite web|title=London Broncos sign Ben Ross and Denny Solomona|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/25776281|website=BBC Sport|access-date=20 September 2014|date=17 January 2014}}</ref> Following the club's relegation, it was announced that he would join [[Castleford Tigers]] at the end of the season.<ref>{{cite web|url=http://www1.skysports.com/rugby-league/news/13995/9381988/super-league-denny-solomona-to-leave-london-broncos-and-join-castleford-tigers|title=Super League: Denny Solomona to leave London Broncos and join Castleford Tigers|website=Sky Sports|date=15 July 2014|access-date=20 September 2014}}</ref>

In 2016, Solomona scored a record-breaking 37th try in a single [[Super League]] season in Castleford's Super 8s win over Catalans. The try broke a 12-year record held by former dual-international, [[Lesley Vainikolo]].<ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.skysports.com/rugby-league/news/12197/10582972/catalans-28-34-castleford-solomona-sets-super-league-try-scoring-record|title=Catalans 28-34 Castleford: Solomona sets Super League try scoring record|date=17 September 2016|access-date=20 September 2016}}</ref> Solomona finished the season with a total of 40 tries along with another 2 in the [[Challenge Cup]] competition.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.skysports.com/rugby-league/news/12196/10593292/castleford-40-26-widnes-denny-solomona-breaks-super-league-record-for-most-tries-in-a-season|title=Castleford 40-26 Widnes: Denny Solomona breaks Super League record for most tries in a season|date=25 September 2016|access-date=7 October 2016}}</ref> On 8 October 2016 Solomona made his International début for [[Samoa national rugby league team|Samoa]] in their historical [[2016 Samoa vs. Fiji rugby league test match|test match]] against Fiji in Apia.

==Rugby Union==
On 13 December 2016, it was confirmed that Solomona had retired from rugby league, with two years still remaining on his contract with [[Castleford Tigers]], to switch to [[rugby union]], and join [[Sale Sharks]] in the [[English Premiership (rugby union)|Aviva Premiership]]. The move was subject to a legal dispute which was concluded out of court as Sale paid Castleford some £200,000 in compensation.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.theguardian.com/sport/2017/jun/16/castleford-sale-settlement-denny-solomona|title=Castleford and Sale reach £200,000 settlement over Denny Solomona|website=[[TheGuardian.com]]|date=16 June 2017}}</ref> On 29 March 2017, having enjoyed a prolific start to his career with Sale (10 tries in 11 appearances), Solomona declared himself available for international selection with [[England national rugby union team|England]].<ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.planetrugby.com/news/solomona-ready-to-play-for-england/|title=Solomona ready to play for England|date=29 March 2017|work=planetrugby.com|access-date=29 March 2017}}</ref> He was subsequently called up to the senior England squad by [[Eddie Jones (rugby union)|Eddie Jones]] for their 2017 summer tour of Argentina and came on as a sub in the opening test and scored the winning try on his debut.l<ref>{{cite news|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-union/39652871|publisher=BBC Sport|title=England v Argentina: Dylan Hartley, Joe Launchbury & George Ford in squad|date=20 April 2017|access-date=20 April 2017}}</ref>

==Influences==
Solomona feels that meeting senior players can be important for the motivation and development of school age players.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.rugbypass.com/news/mental-illness-is-not-something-you-can-switch-on-and-off-its-a-constant-battle-that-you-do-fight-against-solomona-sale/|title = Rugby News | Latest Rugby News & Articles | RugbyPass| date=28 February 2019 }}</ref>

==Post-playing Career==
Solomona left North Harbour Rugby and returned to Wigan in 2023 where he commenced his plumbing apprenticeship. Solomona is the owner of Pasifika Plumbing and Heating <ref>{{cite web | url=https://pasifikaplumbingandheating.co.uk/?fbclid=PAZXh0bgNhZW0CMTEAAaYFnTLQa1nz8I5GCYiNeLvveIyqpJlF49LBrxBGZqfhAGhD1LN8b_zJWNE_aem_aI-CyuOJKYdOpmKCy4mSSQ | title=Home }}</ref>
